next_steps: Perform a percutaneous ultrasound-guided kidney biopsy
Check serum autoantibodies including ANA, anti-dsDNA, and anti-PLA2R
Initiate supportive therapy with ACE inhibitor or ARB for proteinuria and blood pressure control
Evaluate for subclinical hypothyroidism (TSH repeat and TPO antibodies)
Initiate thromboembolic prophylaxis due to severe hypoalbuminemia (nephrotic range proteinuria)

differential_diagnoses: Postpartum preeclampsia with atypical persistent course
next_steps: Perform a percutaneous ultrasound-guided kidney biopsy for definitive histologic diagnosis
Obtain 24-hour urine collection for accurate protein quantification
Check serum autoantibodies including anti-PLA2R, ANA, and anti-dsDNA
Measure serum complement C3 and C4 levels
Order serum protein electrophoresis and immunofixation to exclude monoclonal gammopathy
Initiate ACE inhibitor or ARB for antiproteinuric therapy
Start diuretics for symptomatic edema management
Evaluate for clinical significance of subclinical hypothyroidism with repeat TSH, fT4, and thyroid peroxidase antibodies
Assess risk-benefit for prophylactic anticoagulation due to severe hypoalbuminemia
